references.text: Moody, K. 1986. A literature review of weeds in maize (Zea mays) in the Philippines. Philippine Journal of Weed Science 13: 19-27.
Moody, K. 1989. Weeds reported in rice in South and Southeast Asia. IRRI, Philippines
Ramamoorthy, TP & EM Zardini. 1987. The systematics and evolution of Ludwigia sect. Myrtocarpus sensu lato (Onagraceae). Monogr. Syst. Bot. Missouri Bot. Gard. 19: 1-120.
Raven, PH. 1967. The genus Epilobium in Malesia (Onagraceae). Blumea 15: 269-282
Raven, PH. 1977. Onagraceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 8: 98-113
Turner, IM. 2021. An updated synopsis of Ludwigia (Onagraceae) in Malesia. Reinwardtia 20: 9-16

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 October 2021

species.citation: D.Don , Prodr. Fl. Nepal. (1825) 222 ; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 109. ssp. trichoneurum (Hausskn.) P.H.Raven, Bull. Brit. Mus. (Nat. Hist.), Bot. 2 (1962) 362; Blumea 15 (1967) 271; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 109; ≡ Epilobium trichoneurum Hausskn., Oesterr. Bot. Z. 29 (1879) 54. = Epilobium philippinense C.B.Rob., PJS 5c (1910) 369; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 220; Type: Merrill 4484 (K, iso), Luzon: Mountain Province, Mt Data. Distribution: China, India, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province. Notes: Wet open slopes in the pine region, along streams and by springs. Fl. Jul, Oct. 1400-2100m. Native.
C.B.Rob. , PJS 3c (1908) 210 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 221; Raven, Blumea 15 (1967) 272; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 110; Type: Merrill 4744.
species.distribution: China, Japan, Korea, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province.
species.notes: Chiefly along small streams and about cliffs. Fl. Apr-Jun. 1200-2400m.

species.citation: Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 2 (1788) 564 ; Backer & Bakh.f., Fl. Java 1 (1963) 264.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 14 January 2026

species.citation: (L.) H.Hara , J. Jap. Bot. 28 (1953) 290 ; Raven, Reinwardtia 6 (1963) 387; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 104; Turner, Reinwardtia 20 (2021) 10; ≡ Jussiaea adscendens L. , Mant. Pl. (1767) 69; Type: from India. = Jussiaea repens L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 388 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 219; Backer & Bakh.f., Fl. Java 1 (1963) 260.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, C America, Cambodia, Caribbean, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LEYTE, LUZON: Bulacan (photos), Cagayan, Ifugao, Laguna (photos), NCR, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Maguindanao del Sur, Zamboanga del Sur (photos), SAMAR (photos).
species.notes: Freshwater pools, ditches, swamps, fallow and wet paddies, very common. 20-55m (photos) to 1600m.
Reported from Laguna by Ramamoorthy & Zardini (1987) and listed by Moody (1986, 1989) as a weed in maize and rice in the Philippines, although Turner (2021) did not find herbarium specimens.

species.citation: (G.Don) Exell , Garcia de Orta 5 (1957) 471 ; Raven, Reinwardtia 6 (1963) 387; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 104; Turner, Reinwardtia 20 (2021) 10; ≡ Jussiaea hyssopifolia G.Don , Gen. Hist. 2 (1832) 693. = Jussiaea linifolia Vahl , Eclog. Amer. 2 (1798) 32 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 356; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 219; Backer & Bakh.f., Fl. Java 1 (1963) 260.
species.distribution: BALABAC, BASILAN, BILIRAN, LUZON: Bataan, Ifugao, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, South Cotabato, MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Circumtropical. Sometimes considered native to the Philippines, but invasive outside of its native distribution range (CABI, 2017). Pools, along rice paddies, canals and shallow ditches, river edges, waterlogged wastelands, etc. Lowlands. (?-)1000m.

species.citation: (Jacq.) P.H.Raven , Kew Bull. 15 (1962) 476 ; Reinwardtia 6 (1963) 356; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 101; Turner, Reinwardtia 20 (2021) 11; ≡ Jussiaea octovalvis (Jacq.) Sw. , Observ. Bot. (Swartz) (1791) 142; ≡ Oenothera octovalvis Jacq. , Enum. Syst. Pl. (1760) 19. = Jussiaea villosa Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 3 (1789) 331 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 220; Type: from India. = Jussiaea erecta L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 388 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 219. ≠ Jussiaea suffruticosa auct. non L. ; Miq., Fl. Ind. Bat. 11 (1856) 628; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 357.
species.distribution: Africa, America, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, Caribbean, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Moluccas, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. BALABAC, BASILAN, BILIRAN, BUSUANGA, LUZON: Apayao, Bataan, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga (photos), Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, South Cotabato, Zamboanga del Norte, NEGROS, PALAWAN (photos), PANAY, ROMBLON, SAMAR, SIASI, TABLAS.
species.notes: Waterlogged sites, pools, rice fields, along water-courses, lake shores, also gravelly riverbeds, etc. (?-)100-200m (photos) to 1000-2100m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 119 ; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 103; Reinwardtia 6 (1963) 367; Turner, Reinwardtia 20 (2021) 13. = Ludwigia parviflora Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 440 ; Hort. Beng. (1814) 11, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 355; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 220.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, Hong Kong, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam, W Asia. BALABAC, LUZON: Laguna, NCR, MINDANAO: Davao, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Sunny waterlogged conditions, along fallow rice-fields, watercourses, riverbeds, on heavy clay, etc. Fl. throughout the year. (?-)500m.

species.citation: Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 441 ; Hort. Beng. (1814) 11, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 355; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 220; Raven, Reinwardtia 6 (1963) 374; Raven, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 103; Turner, Reinwardtia 20 (2021) 14.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, NEGROS, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Lowlands to c. 800m, along rice paddies, ditches, river banks, swampy sites, rather rare. (?-)800m.

species.citation: (Bonpl.) H.Hara , J. Jap. Bot. 28 (1953) 294; ≡ Jussiaea sedioides Bonpl. , Pl. Aequinoct. 1 (1805) 13.
species.notes: John Edward Matulina Villasis (2-Feb-25): Widely cultivated in the country for aquascaping. Sometimes planted in artificial ponds within settlements but not known to grow in natural bodies of water.
